article_title: Yale Astronomers Discover a Third Galaxy Missing Its Dark Matter, Strengthening the Case for Violent Cosmic Collisions

content_preview.title: Yale Astronomers Discover a Third Galaxy Missing Its Dark Matter, Strengthening the Case for Violent Cosmic Collisions
content_preview.summary: NGC 1052-DF9 is the third ultra-diffuse galaxy found lacking dark matter in a linear trail near NGC 1052, lending significant support to the Bullet Dwarf collision theory and challenging alternative gravity models.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A team of Yale astronomers has identified a third galaxy in the NGC 1052 field that appears to contain little or no dark matter, adding to a growing body of evidence that supports a dramatic theory about how some galaxies form. The discovery of NGC 1052-DF9, detailed in a [preprint published on arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15860) by Michael A. Keim, Pieter van Dokkum, Zili Shen, Shany Danieli, and Imad Pasha, strengthens the so-called Bullet Dwarf collision scenario and poses a...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent astrophysics article with clear explanation of Bullet Dwarf theory and MOND implications.
editor_notes.source_verification: Phys.org: verified — NGC 1052-DF9, third dark-matter-deficient galaxy, linear trail, Yale/Keim/van Dokkum, MOND challenge confirmed. Universe Today and arXiv provide supplementary data.
